passages.title: The Power of Perseverance
passages.content:
Thomas Edison, one of the greatest inventors in history, is famous for his invention of the light bulb. However, what many people don't know is how many times he failed before he succeeded. It is said that Edison tried more than 1,000 different materials before finding the right one for the filament of the light bulb.

When a reporter asked him how he felt about failing so many times, Edison replied, "I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work." This simple but powerful statement reveals Edison's remarkable attitude toward failure. He didn't view unsuccessful attempts as failures but as steps toward success.

This attitude is something we can all learn from. In our studies and in life, we will face difficulties and setbacks. The key is not to avoid failure — which is impossible — but to learn from it and keep going. Every mistake we make teaches us something valuable.

Research has shown that successful people in all fields share one common characteristic: perseverance. They continue working toward their goals even when things get difficult. They understand that success is rarely achieved overnight. Instead, it is the result of consistent effort over a long period.

So next time you face a challenge, remember Edison's words. Your "failure" might just be another step on the path to success.

questions.question: What is Thomas Edison famous for?
questions.explanation: 文章第一句明确提到Edison因发明电灯泡而闻名。

questions.question: How did Edison view his failed attempts?
options: As proof he should give up
questions.explanation: Edison说"I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work"，把失败看作通向成功的步骤。

questions.question: According to the passage, what characteristic do successful people share?
questions.explanation: 文章明确提到"successful people in all fields share one common characteristic: perseverance."

questions.question: What does the word 'setbacks' in paragraph 3 most probably mean?
questions.explanation: 从上下文看，setbacks与difficulties并列，意为"挫折、困难"。

questions.question: What is the main message of this passage?
options: Never give up in the face of failure
The light bulb is an important invention
questions.answer: Never give up in the face of failure
questions.explanation: 全文通过Edison的例子传达的核心观点是：面对失败不要放弃，坚持下去。

passages.title: The Rise of E-books
passages.content:
Over the past decade, the way people read has changed dramatically. With the development of digital technology, e-books have become increasingly popular around the world. Many people now prefer reading on electronic devices such as tablets and e-readers rather than traditional paper books.

There are several reasons for this trend. First, e-books are convenient. A single device can store thousands of books, making it easy for readers to carry their entire library wherever they go. Second, e-books are often cheaper than printed books, which makes reading more affordable for many people. Third, e-readers offer features that printed books cannot match, such as adjustable font sizes, built-in dictionaries, and the ability to search for specific content instantly.

However, printed books still have their supporters. Many readers say they enjoy the physical experience of holding a book, turning pages, and even the smell of paper. Studies have also suggested that reading printed books may help people remember information better than reading on screens. Additionally, looking at screens for long periods can cause eye strain and affect sleep quality.

Perhaps the future is not about choosing one over the other. Many people now use both formats, depending on the situation. They might read e-books while traveling and printed books at home. This combination allows them to enjoy the advantages of both formats. What matters most is not the format but the habit of reading itself.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of e-books mentioned in the passage?
questions.explanation: 文章提到"A single device can store thousands of books, making it easy for readers to carry their entire library."

questions.question: What is one disadvantage of reading on screens?
questions.explanation: 文章提到"looking at screens for long periods can cause eye strain."

questions.question: What do some studies suggest about printed books?
questions.explanation: 文章提到"reading printed books may help people remember information better than reading on screens."

questions.question: What is the author's opinion about the future of reading?
questions.explanation: 作者认为未来不是二选一，人们会根据情况使用不同格式。

questions.question: According to the passage, what matters most about reading?
questions.explanation: 文章最后一句"What matters most is not the format but the habit of reading itself."

passages.title: Volunteering Abroad
passages.content:
More and more young people are choosing to volunteer abroad during their gap year or summer holidays. This trend reflects a growing desire among young people to make a positive difference in the world while gaining valuable life experience.

Volunteering abroad offers many benefits. Firstly, it allows volunteers to experience different cultures firsthand. By living and working with local people, volunteers gain a deeper understanding of the world's diversity. Secondly, volunteering helps develop important skills such as communication, problem-solving, and teamwork. These skills are highly valued by universities and employers. Thirdly, the experience of helping others can be deeply rewarding and may inspire volunteers to pursue careers in fields like education, healthcare, or social work.

However, volunteering abroad is not without its challenges. Volunteers may face language barriers, culture shock, and difficult living conditions. Some projects may not be well-organized, and volunteers might feel that their efforts are not making a real difference. There is also criticism that "voluntourism" — where volunteering becomes more about the volunteer's experience than about helping the local community — can sometimes do more harm than good.

To make the most of a volunteering experience, experts recommend doing thorough research before choosing a program. Look for organizations that work closely with local communities and have a clear, sustainable mission. Remember that the goal is not just to feel good about yourself but to contribute in a meaningful way to the community you are serving.

questions.question: Why do young people choose to volunteer abroad?
options: To make a difference and gain experience
questions.answer: To make a difference and gain experience
questions.explanation: 文章第一段提到年轻人想做积极贡献同时获得宝贵人生经验。

questions.question: Which is NOT mentioned as a benefit of volunteering abroad?
questions.explanation: 文章提到的三个好处是：体验文化、发展技能、获得有意义的体验，没有提到高薪。

questions.question: What does the term 'voluntourism' refer to?
options: Volunteering that focuses more on the volunteer's experience
questions.answer: Volunteering that focuses more on the volunteer's experience
questions.explanation: 文章解释voluntourism是"where volunteering becomes more about the volunteer's experience than about helping the local community."

questions.question: What do experts suggest before choosing a volunteer program?
questions.explanation: 文章最后建议"doing thorough research before choosing a program."

questions.question: What is the author's overall attitude toward volunteering abroad?
options: Balanced, showing both benefits and challenges
questions.answer: Balanced, showing both benefits and challenges
questions.explanation: 文章客观地介绍了海外志愿服务的利与弊，态度是平衡的。
